page: Amazon Web Services m8id.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs derived from 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of 11,400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, which supports high-throughput tasks like static web serving and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core results falling in the middle 50% tier. The CPU includes Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which drive top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds for smaller models scale down to average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.48xlarge offers high resource density by combining massive compute capacity, high-speed DDR5 memory, and substantial local NVMe storage on a single instance. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-I/O workloads, optimizing resource-to-cost ratios for data-intensive operations. The instance is highly suited for large-scale database operations, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and local LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs from 96 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance remains in the average tier. The processor supports AVX-512 and AMX extensions, enabling top-tier prompt processing for large language models. With its high-capacity local storage and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this instance is optimized for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: A high-density general-purpose platform combining 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs with massive local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory.

768 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
11,400 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier multi-core, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark performance

Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and LLM prompt processing
